ksa_text: Manage consent lifecycle across organizational systems, including collection configuration, preference storage, withdrawal processing, re-consent campaign execution, and audit record maintenance.

dcwf.top_element_label: Knowledge of the acquisition/procurement life cycle process.

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Knowledge of the acquisition/procurement life cycle process.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Regulatory Compliance falls outside the primary competency scope of the Director of Data Products. This task KSA does not align with the role's core responsibilities and is excluded.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope, seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with exclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Is Regulatory Compliance truly out-of-scope for the Director of Data Products? Emerging role definitions may include broader competency expectations.
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Current job market analysis and framework definitions (DCWF, DDAT, NICE) do not list Regulatory Compliance as a core or recommended competency for the Director of Data Products. Role scope exclusion is consistent with external evidence.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: external evidence confirms Regulatory Compliance falls outside the established competency profile for the Director of Data Products. Exclusion maintained.

decision_rationale: Regulatory Compliance is outside the competency scope of the Director of Data Products. All evaluation passes consistently support exclusion.
